keyRules: Maximum height: 120 m (400 ft) above ground level
Minimum distance of 9.2 km from controlled airports
Minimum distance of 3.7 km from uncontrolled aerodromes
Minimum distance of 900 m from heliports
Always keep the drone within direct line of sight (VLOS), no more than 457 m from the operator
Drones weighing 250 g or more: mandatory registration with the AFAC (Appendix K form)
Drones weighing less than 250 g: exempt from registration, but all other rules apply
Weight categories: Micro (≤2 kg), Light (2–25 kg), Heavy (>25 kg)
Night flights require specific authorization from the AFAC
Commercial use requires additional authorization depending on the weight category
importantNotes: ⚠️ FOREIGN PILOTS: NOM-107 prohibits the operation of drones registered abroad or by foreign nationals unless there is a bilateral agreement between Mexico and the country of origin. Tourists: Check before you travel.
Penalties for illegal operations can range up to MXN $80,000 or imprisonment
National parks and archaeological sites (INAH) generally prohibit drones without special permission
Marinas and coastal areas are often subject to restrictions—check with local authorities
The AFAC registry has a response time of up to 10 business days—plan ahead

primaryLinks.title: AFAC — Rules for Drones (RPAS)
primaryLinks.description: Official website of the Federal Civil Aviation Agency, featuring regulations, forms, and drone registration

primaryLinks.title: AFAC — Drone Registration (Appendix K)
primaryLinks.description: Register your drone using the form in Appendix K. Send it to rpas@afac.gob.mx — you will receive a response within 10 business days

primaryLinks.title: NOM-107-SCT3-2019 — Official Standard
primaryLinks.description: Official Mexican Standard establishing the requirements for operating a remotely piloted aircraft system

primaryLinks.title: Circular CO AV-23/10 R4
primaryLinks.description: Mandatory circular setting forth detailed technical and operational requirements for drones

secondaryLinks.title: ASA — Airports and Auxiliary Services
secondaryLinks.description: Information on Mexican federal airports to check for restricted areas

secondaryLinks.title: SICT — Civil Aviation
secondaryLinks.description: Ministry of Infrastructure, Communications, and Transportation — AFAC's governing authority

secondaryLinks.title: AFAC — Direct Contact (RPAS)
secondaryLinks.description: AFAC phone number for RPAS inquiries: (55) 5723-9300, ext. 18148, 18125, 18115

fields.disclaimerText: Always check AFAC's official sources before flying. This app is a tool, not a legal authority. Mexico enforces NOM-107-SCT3-2019, and penalties are imposed by the Federal Civil Aviation Agency.
